questions.q: Indira Gandhi holds which distinction in the office of Prime Minister of India?
options: First and only woman Prime Minister to date
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the first and, to date, only woman to serve as Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi was the daughter of which Indian leader?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the daughter of Jawaharlal Nehru, India's first Prime Minister.

questions.q: In which year did Indira Gandhi first become Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi first became Prime Minister in 1966.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi's first continuous term as Prime Minister ran from 1966 until which year?
questions.explanation: Her first continuous tenure as Prime Minister lasted from 1966 to 1977.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi returned to the office of Prime Minister again in which year?
questions.explanation: She returned as Prime Minister in 1980 for a second period in office.

questions.q: Whom did Indira Gandhi succeed when she first became Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: She became Prime Minister in 1966 following the death of Lal Bahadur Shastri.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi's second period as Prime Minister, beginning in 1980, ended in which year?
questions.explanation: Her second period in office ran from 1980 until 1984.

questions.q: Before becoming Prime Minister, Indira Gandhi held which cabinet portfolio under Lal Bahadur Shastri?
questions.explanation: She served as Minister of Information and Broadcasting before becoming Prime Minister.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi was a member of which political party?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was a leader of the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: In total, how many separate periods did Indira Gandhi serve as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: She served as Prime Minister in two separate periods, 1966-1977 and 1980-1984.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi was Prime Minister during the war that led to the creation of which new nation in 1971?
questions.explanation: She was Prime Minister during the 1971 war that led to the formation of Bangladesh.

questions.q: Which major banking measure was taken by Indira Gandhi's government in 1969?
questions.explanation: Her government nationalised several major commercial banks in 1969.

questions.q: India conducted its first nuclear test in 1974 during whose tenure as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: India's first nuclear test was carried out in 1974 while Indira Gandhi was Prime Minister.

questions.q: A national Emergency was proclaimed in 1975 during the tenure of which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: The Emergency of 1975 was proclaimed while Indira Gandhi was Prime Minister.

questions.q: Who became Prime Minister immediately after Indira Gandhi's first tenure ended in 1977?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai became Prime Minister in 1977, after the 1977 general election.

questions.q: Who succeeded Indira Gandhi as Prime Minister in 1984?
questions.explanation: Rajiv Gandhi became Prime Minister in 1984, succeeding Indira Gandhi.

questions.q: The 'Garibi Hatao' slogan is associated with which Prime Minister's election campaign?
questions.explanation: The 'Garibi Hatao' (Remove Poverty) slogan was associated with Indira Gandhi's campaign.

questions.q: The merger of which former Himalayan kingdom into India as a state took place in 1975 during Indira Gandhi's tenure?
questions.explanation: Sikkim became a state of India in 1975 during Indira Gandhi's tenure as Prime Minister.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi's two periods in office together span roughly how many years as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Her two terms, 1966-1977 and 1980-1984, together total about fifteen years in office.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi's married surname, 'Gandhi', came from her husband, who was named what?
questions.explanation: Her surname came from her husband, Feroze Gandhi, and was unrelated to Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi had earlier served as President of which organisation in 1959?
questions.explanation: She served as President of the Indian National Congress in 1959.

questions.q: During Indira Gandhi's tenure, the words 'socialist' and 'secular' were added to the Preamble by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment of 1976, during her tenure, added 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble.

questions.q: In which month and year did Indira Gandhi first take office as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: She first took office as Prime Minister in January 1966.

questions.q: Indira Gandhi represented which Lok Sabha constituency for much of her parliamentary career?
questions.explanation: She represented the Rae Bareli constituency in the Lok Sabha for much of her career.

questions.q: The Simla Agreement of 1972 was signed between India, led by Indira Gandhi, and which neighbouring country?
questions.explanation: The Simla Agreement of 1972 was signed between India and Pakistan following the 1971 war.

questions.q: In which year was Indira Gandhi awarded the Bharat Ratna?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 1971.

questions.q: The 20-Point Programme, a set of socio-economic objectives, was associated with which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: The 20-Point Programme was launched under Indira Gandhi's government.

questions.q: In which year was Indira Gandhi born?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was born in 1917 in Allahabad.

questions.q: The 44th Amendment, passed in 1978 after her first tenure, modified emergency provisions enacted during which Prime Minister's term?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment revisited emergency-related provisions associated with Indira Gandhi's earlier tenure.

questions.q: Operation Flood, the major dairy development programme, expanded significantly during the tenure of which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: The Operation Flood dairy programme expanded substantially during Indira Gandhi's years in office.
